cdp.name: CDP
cdp.gloss: Customer Data Platform — the unified store of who your customers are.

perso.name: Personalization (1:1)
perso.gloss: Tailoring the message to the individual, not the segment.

genai.name: Generative AI
genai.gloss: Models that produce content — copy, images, replies — from a prompt.

agentic.name: Agentic AI
agentic.gloss: Systems that don't just generate, they decide and act toward a goal.

decis.name: Agentic decisioning
decis.gloss: An agent choosing what to say, when, how often and on which channel — per person.

bandit.name: Bandits / RL
bandit.gloss: The maths that lets a system learn which choice wins by trying and measuring.

cep.name: CEP
cep.gloss: Customer Engagement Platform — where this messaging actually runs.

geo.name: GEO
geo.gloss: Generative Engine Optimization — being visible inside AI answers.

govern.name: Governance
govern.gloss: The controls that make automated decisions safe, explainable and legible.

stories.head: Your newest competitors aren't other brands — GEO and AI authority explained
stories.plain: AI systems are shaping buyer opinions before people even visit a brand's website, making visibility inside AI-generated answers a new competitive battleground. This piece explains how marketers must build authority across the sources AI trusts — a fundamental shift in how brand discovery and consideration work.

stories.head: Bloomreach survey: 80% of shoppers say AI shopping experiences met or exceeded expectations
stories.plain: A 4,000-person US/UK consumer survey finds AI shopping tools have crossed from novelty to necessity, with most respondents saying AI experiences already meet their expectations. This shifts the conversation for MarTech buyers: personalization AI is no longer a nice-to-have experiment — customers are actively choosing brands that offer it.

stories.head: The missing layer behind customer data ROI: why CDPs struggle to unify fragmented data
stories.plain: Many CDPs are good at activating data but fail at the harder job of unifying messy, fragmented customer records — a gap called the 'silver layer.' This matters because poor unification means personalization and decisioning built on top of the CDP will act on bad data, undermining the entire stack.

stories.head: Is your website invisible to AI search? What brands need to fix now
stories.plain: AI crawlers read web content differently from traditional search bots, and many brand sites that look polished to humans are effectively invisible to AI-powered search engines. The article gives actionable fixes — directly relevant to any marketer whose budget includes organic discovery or content strategy.

stories.head: RocketReach MCP Server gives AI assistants real-time access to 700M+ verified contacts
stories.plain: RocketReach is connecting its contact intelligence database directly to AI assistants like ChatGPT and Claude via the Model Context Protocol (MCP), letting sales and marketing agents prospect in real time without leaving their AI workflow. This is an early example of how data vendors are rewiring themselves for an agentic AI stack.

stories.head: Salesforce's Agentforce adoption woes reveal marketing's agentic AI readiness gap
stories.plain: Slow uptake of Salesforce Agentforce shows that poor data quality and operational unreadiness are the real blockers to enterprise agentic AI — not the technology itself. This matters to buyers and builders alike: no amount of AI capability rescues a dirty, siloed data foundation, making this a governance and CDP story as much as an AI one.

stories.head: Stop buying AI and start buying capabilities: a four-part vendor evaluation framework
stories.plain: The article argues that 'AI-powered' is a marketing label, not a specification, and gives buyers a four-part framework to decode what a product actually does before signing a contract. For anyone new to MarTech procurement, this is a practical cheat-sheet for cutting through vendor noise and asking the right questions.

stories.head: Bloomreach survey: 80% of shoppers say AI shopping experiences have met or exceeded expectations
stories.plain: A 4,000-person U.S./U.K. survey finds consumers now prefer AI-driven shopping tools over traditional search and browsing, and adoption has flipped from novelty to routine in under a year. This shifts the business case for personalization investment from 'nice to have' to a competitive necessity that directly affects purchase behavior.

stories.head: Is your website invisible to AI search? What brands must do about AI crawlers
stories.plain: Many brand sites that look fine in a browser appear as empty pages to AI crawlers powering generative search answers. The piece explains the technical gaps — missing structured data, JavaScript rendering issues — and what to fix. For marketers, being invisible to AI answers means losing buyers before they ever visit your site.

stories.head: The missing layer behind customer data ROI: why CDPs struggle with fragmented data unification
stories.plain: CDPs are good at activating data but frequently fail at the harder job of unifying fragmented customer records before activation. The article identifies a 'silver layer' of data preparation that determines whether a CDP actually pays off — a foundational concept for anyone evaluating or implementing a customer data stack.

month.skicker: 30-day synthesis · the shape underneath the feed

themes.head: AI Agents Need Live Data Pipelines to Act
body: A cluster of launches this month — RocketReach MCP giving agents access to 700M+ contacts, Guideline's Ad Intelligence MCP Server, and the broader Model Context Protocol wave — signals that AI agents are only as useful as the real-time data they can reach. The bottleneck is no longer the model; it is the structured, permissioned feed connecting the model to live business data.

For marketing and revenue teams, this means the next procurement decision isn't 'which AI tool?' but 'which data connectors does it support?' MCP-compatible data sources are becoming the new integration standard, and vendors without them risk being bypassed entirely by agentic workflows.
ev: MarTech Series · Guideline Ad Intelligence MCP

themes.head: The Experimentation-to-Transformation Gap Is Widening
body: West Monroe research and multiple MarTech editorials converge on the same uncomfortable finding: most enterprises are running dozens of AI pilots but transforming nothing. The divide between teams that have integrated AI into core workflows and those still experimenting is growing faster than the tools are improving.

The practical implication is that buying more AI licenses does not close the gap — MarTech's 'stop buying AI, start buying capabilities' framing makes this explicit. CMOs and CIOs need a capability-acquisition strategy, not a vendor-collection strategy, or the investment stays stuck in proof-of-concept forever.
ev: MarTech · What CMOs should do now

themes.head: AI Search Is Rewriting Competitive Sets
body: Two MarTech pieces this month reframe brand competition in the AI era: your newest rivals are AI-generated answers and recommendation surfaces, not other brands in your category. If your content isn't structured for AI retrieval, you are invisible to a growing share of purchase-intent queries — the 'is your website invisible to AI search?' piece quantifies exactly that risk.

This forces an immediate strategic question about content architecture and structured data. Brands optimised for legacy SEO may rank well on Google and be entirely absent from AI-mediated discovery, which is already where Bloomreach's consumer survey shows shoppers prefer to start. The window to act before habits calcify is short.
ev: MarTech Series · Shoppers prefer AI tools

themes.head: Agentic AI in Production Is Messier Than Promised
body: Salesforce's publicised struggles with its agentic AI rollout became a case study the industry couldn't ignore. The MarTech analysis makes clear that orchestrating autonomous agents inside real enterprise environments — with fragmented data, legacy permissions, and compliance constraints — is dramatically harder than demo conditions suggest. redSling's launch of a 'sovereign' agentic platform signals that data-sovereignty and governance are now table-stakes requirements, not afterthoughts.

The unified-workflows piece reinforces the same lesson: agents fail at the seams between systems, not within them. Enterprises that haven't unified their underlying workflow layer before deploying agents will get agents that hallucinate at handoff points — a risk that erodes trust faster than any headline.
ev: MarTech Series · redSling Zenith sovereign platform

themes.head: Human Judgment as the Irreducible Differentiator
body: Across practitioner commentary this month — MERGE CEO Stephanie Trunzo on human elements in transformation, the 'invisible customer experience' framing, and the value-migration piece — a counter-narrative to full automation is forming. The highest-value marketing work is now defined by what AI cannot yet reliably do: contextual judgment, ethical framing, and relationship trust. Marketers who cede those tasks lose their clearest point of differentiation.

This isn't a soft argument against automation; it is a resource-allocation signal. As AI handles information retrieval and content assembly, human time should concentrate on insight synthesis and strategic decisions — exactly the shift the Agile Brand's 'information to insight' piece argues is the core value migration marketers must master right now.
ev: Agile Brand · MERGE CEO on human elements
Agile Brand · Information to insight migration
Agile Brand · Best CX is invisible

month.takeaway: The month's news collectively shows that the real AI transformation barrier is no longer the model — it is the data plumbing, workflow integration, and human judgment layer that determines whether any of it compounds into durable competitive advantage.
